[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Symarip%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Symarip%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Symarip%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Symarip%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(September 2008)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(also known at various stages of their career as **The Bees**, **The Pyramids**, **Seven Letters** and **Zubaba**) were a British [ska](/wiki/Ska "Ska") and [reggae](/wiki/Reggae "Reggae") [band](/wiki/Music_ensemble "Music ensemble"), originating in the late 1960s, when Frank Pitter and Michael Thomas founded the band as The Bees. The band's name was originally spelled *Simaryp*, which is an approximate reversal of the word *pyramids*.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Consisting of members of [West Indian](/wiki/Caribbean "Caribbean") descent, Simaryp is widely marked as one of the first [skinhead](/wiki/Skinhead "Skinhead") reggae bands, being one of the first to target skinheads as an audience. Their [hits](/wiki/Hit_record "Hit record") included "Skinhead Girl", "Skinhead Jamboree" and "[Skinhead Moonstomp](/wiki/Skinhead_Moonstomp "Skinhead Moonstomp")", the latter based on the [Derrick Morgan](/wiki/Derrick_Morgan "Derrick Morgan") song, "[Moon Hop](/wiki/Moon_Hop "Moon Hop")".[[2]](#cite_note-Larkin-2)

["Symarip Sleeve Notes"](https://web.archive.org/web/20050906102048/http://www.trojanrecords.net/articles/symarip/sleevenotes.htm). 6 September 2005. Archived from [the original](http://www.trojanrecords.net/articles/symarip/sleevenotes.htm) on 6 September 2005.

[Colin Larkin](/wiki/Colin_Larkin_(writer) "Colin Larkin (writer)"), ed. (1997). *[The Virgin Encyclopedia of Popular Music](/wiki/Encyclopedia_of_Popular_Music "Encyclopedia of Popular Music")* (Concise ed.). [Virgin Books](/wiki/Virgin_Books "Virgin Books"). p. 984. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-85227-745-9](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-85227-745-9 "Special:BookSources/1-85227-745-9").

Roberts, David (2006). *British Hit Singles & Albums* (19th ed.). London: Guinness World Records Limited. p. 544. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-904994-10-5](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-904994-10-5 "Special:BookSources/1-904994-10-5").

["Official Charts Company - Symarip"](https://www.officialcharts.com/artists/). [Official Charts Company](/wiki/Official_Charts_Company "Official Charts Company"). 19 April 2012. Retrieved 23 April 2012.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

British ska and reggae band

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Symarip "Special:EditPage/Symarip") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners") in this article. Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Symarip"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Symarip%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Symarip%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Symarip%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Symarip%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Symarip%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Symarip%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(September 2008)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ae/Mr._Symarip_Klub-Ost_03.jpg/250px-Mr._Symarip_Klub-Ost_03.jpg)](/wiki/File:Mr._Symarip_Klub-Ost_03.jpg)

Mr. Symarip at a concert in Klub-Ost, Vienna

**Symarip** (also known at various stages of their career as **The Bees**, **The Pyramids**, **Seven Letters** and **Zubaba**) were a British [ska](/wiki/Ska "Ska") and [reggae](/wiki/Reggae "Reggae") [band](/wiki/Music_ensemble "Music ensemble"), originating in the late 1960s, when Frank Pitter and Michael Thomas founded the band as The Bees. The band's name was originally spelled *Simaryp*, which is an approximate reversal of the word *pyramids*.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Consisting of members of [West Indian](/wiki/Caribbean "Caribbean") descent, Simaryp is widely marked as one of the first [skinhead](/wiki/Skinhead "Skinhead") reggae bands, being one of the first to target skinheads as an audience. Their [hits](/wiki/Hit_record "Hit record") included "Skinhead Girl", "Skinhead Jamboree" and "[Skinhead Moonstomp](/wiki/Skinhead_Moonstomp "Skinhead Moonstomp")", the latter based on the [Derrick Morgan](/wiki/Derrick_Morgan "Derrick Morgan") song, "[Moon Hop](/wiki/Moon_Hop "Moon Hop")".[[2]](#cite_note-Larkin-2)

They moved to Germany in 1971, performing reggae and [Afro-rock](/wiki/Afro-rock "Afro-rock") under the name Zubaba. In 1980, the single "Skinhead Moonstomp" was re-issued in the wake of the [2 Tone](/wiki/2_Tone_(music_genre) "2 Tone (music genre)") craze, hitting No. 54 on the [UK Singles Chart](/wiki/UK_Singles_Chart "UK Singles Chart").[[3]](#cite_note-British_Hit_Singles_&_Albums-3)[[4]](#cite_note-chartstats-4) The band officially split in 1985 after releasing the album *Drunk & Disorderly* as The Pyramids. The album was released by [Ariola Records](/wiki/Ariola_Records "Ariola Records") and was produced by [Stevie B](/wiki/Stevie_B "Stevie B").

Pitter and Ellis moved back to England, where Ellis continued performing as a solo artist, sometimes using the stage name 'Mr. Symarip'. Mike Thomas met a Finnish woman while living in Switzerland and relocated to Finland doing the groundwork for the Finnish reggae culture through his band 'Mike T. Saganor'. Monty Neysmith moved to the United States, where he toured as a solo artist.

In 2004, [Trojan Records](/wiki/Trojan_Records "Trojan Records") released a [best of](/wiki/Greatest_hits "Greatest hits") album including a new single by Neysmith and Ellis, "Back From the Moon". In 2005, Neysmith and Ellis performed together at Club Ska in England, and a recording of the concert was released on [Moon Ska Records](/wiki/Moon_Ska_Records "Moon Ska Records") as *Symarip – Live at Club Ska*. In April 2008, they headlined the Ska Splash Festival in [Lincolnshire](/wiki/Lincolnshire "Lincolnshire") as Symarip, and later performed at the Endorse-It and Fordham Festivals. Pitter and Thomas now perform in a different band as *Symarip Pyramid*. Their Back From The Moon Tour 2008–2009 was with [The Pioneers](/wiki/The_Pioneers_(band) "The Pioneers (band)"). In 2009, to celebrate the rebirth of the band and the reunion of the two original members, Trojan Records released a compilation album, *Ultimate Collection*. Pitter holds all copyright and trademark rights for the name 'Symarip Pyramid'.

## Line-up

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Symarip&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Line-up")]

* Roy Ellis – singer, [trombone](/wiki/Trombone "Trombone") (1969–1985, 2019)
* Josh Roberts – guitar (1969–1985)
* Michael "Mik" Thomas – bass guitar (1969 – 1985, 2008 – present)
* Frank Pitter – drums (1969 – 1985, 2008 – present)
* Monty Neysmith – keyboards, including [Hammond organ](/wiki/Hammond_organ "Hammond organ") (1969 – 1985, 2010 – present)
* Roy Bug Knight – saxophone (2008 – present)
* Johney Johnson – trumpet (2008 – present)
* Carl Grifith – [tenor](/wiki/Tenor_saxophone "Tenor saxophone") and [alto saxophone](/wiki/Alto_saxophone "Alto saxophone") (2008 – present)
